raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, my family and I arrived in XXXX for our stay at XXXX XXXX. There were four of us total : two adults and two children. 

On XX/XX/year>, we attended a sales presentation for the XXXX XXXX membership program. During that presentation, we were subjected to heavy sales pressure and were pushed into signing a membership agreement for approximately {$34000.00}. 

During that same sales process, the sales staff also pushed a so-called free presidential suite upgrade for our current stay. I did not request that upgrade. It was presented to me as free. At the same time I was signing the membership paperwork, they also pushed a separate small document related to that suite . That document stated that the presidential suite would only be free if the membership remained active, and that if the membership was canceled, I would owe approximately {$14000.00} for that suite . 

On XX/XX/year>, within less than 24 hours of signing, I canceled the membership and demanded a full refund. At the same time, I also clearly stated that I was declining the presidential suite , that I did not want any keys made, and that we were fine remaining in our original room. I did not want the suite , did not accept it, and did not use it. 

After that cancellation, XXXX XXXX did not properly resolve the matter or provide the refund I requested. Because of that, I disputed the charge with American Express. 

After I opened the dispute, American Express initially issued a provisional credit of approximately {$34000.00} back to my account. 
Later, XXXX XXXX challenged the dispute and submitted defective documentation to American Express. The document they relied on for the alleged presidential suite charge was not valid. It showed a reservation number of XXXX which indicates there was no real booking. In addition, the document showed only two adults, even though our stay involved four people total : two adults and two children. These inaccuracies show that the document does not reflect a real reservation or an actual upgraded stay. 

I never stayed in a presidential suite , never used one, never accepted one, and specifically declined it when I canceled the membership. Even so, American Express ultimately ruled against me and accepted the merchants defective documentation instead of properly considering the facts, my cancellation, and the inaccuracies in the documents. 

I am filing this complaint because I canceled the membership within 24 hours, rejected the suite , did not receive or use the alleged upgraded accommodation, and American Express failed to fairly evaluate the evidence I provided.
